summary: DHH argues that Google is fundamentally untrustworthy as a product and service company because its business model is built entirely around advertising. He uses the sudden killing of Google Domains—a $180M/year business serving 10 million domains—as the latest evidence. He catalogs Google's pattern of abandoning products like Nest Secure and neglecting others like Google Home, arguing that even enterprise offerings like Workspaces and Google Cloud Platform are perpetually at risk. His conclusion: anything made by Google implicitly carries the disclaimer that it will be abandoned when Google loses interest.
key_points: Google is institutionally incapable of sustaining products that don't directly generate ad revenue at massive scale
Google Domains was killed after nine years despite being a $180M/year business with 10 million customers, proving no product is safe
Google's customer service is uniquely awful because helping product users is never as profitable as helping advertisers
The Nest/Google Home portfolio demonstrates how Google can acquire successful products and make them worse through neglect
Even enterprise offerings like Workspaces and Google Cloud Platform are vulnerable to internal reorganization or executive whims
money_quotes: Google will eventually kill every single service you care about, if they can't find a way to directly monetize it with ads at a scale of billions.
They're institutionally incapable of being in the product or service business, because neither products nor services butter Google's bread. Advertisement does.

Google has always been uniquely awful when it comes to customer service, because helping someone with a problem on Workspaces or even the Google Cloud Platform was never going to be as profitable as helping an advertiser carpet bomb your attention span.
This company is one major reorganization or recently promoted executive away from murdering any of these.
Anything that reads "Made by Google" implicitly has the subscript "until we don't give a fuck any more" printed below.
key_insight: Google's advertising monoculture makes it structurally incapable of sustaining any product or service that doesn't generate ad revenue at scale, so users should always have a backup plan.
